channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: SNK Announces Two Metal Slug Projects in the Works
item.content:encoded:
The Metal Slug development team has announced two new projects as they continue to celebrate the 30th anniversary of the franchise.

The Metal Slug franchise first arrived in 1996, releasing on the Neo Geo. It would later be ported to the Sega Saturn and Sony PlayStation. Over the years, there have been more than a dozen titles in the franchise, including seven mainline games. Most recently, Metal Slug Tactics was released in late 2024 for PC, the Nintendo Switch, the Sony PlayStation 4 and 5, the Xbox One, and the Xbox Series family of consoles.

Now, two new projects are in the works, including a brand-new mobile game and another brand-new experience about which few details are known.

The announcement came from the official Metal Slug accounts on social media. It was revealed that Metal Slug Rush is the name of the new mobile game. It is being released by RingGames under an official license from SNK. The second title doesn’t even reveal a name. All that was said was that it’s a new experience and that the reboot mission celebrating the game’s anniversary is far from over.

Dear METAL SLUG Fans, Thank you for your support—burning as hot as a Flame Shot!

Today, as part of the METAL SLUG 30th Anniversary celebrations, we are pleased to announce the mobile game METAL SLUG RUSH. This title is being released by RingGames under an official license from SNK.

At the same time, our development team is hard at work on another title: a brand-new METAL SLUG! We hope you’ll enjoy METAL SLUG RUSH and stay tuned for future METAL SLUG updates.

The “MISSION REBOOT!” campaign is far from over, and there is much more to come as we celebrate 30 years of METAL SLUG. Thank you for being part of the METAL SLUG 30th Anniversary.

The METAL SLUG Development Team

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. How do you feel about the Metal Slug series? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/snk-announces-two-metal-slug-projects-in-the-works/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Top Football (or Soccer) Games To Play During The World Cup 2026
item.content:encoded:
In case you didn’t know, the World Cup is here and in full flow, and it will be so until June 19. The action has well and truly kicked off, and for the next month or so, the planet is football (not soccer) mad. That being said, sporting fans the world over are looking for the top football games to play during the World Cup to keep them in good spirits.

If you’ve been hunting the top soccer games (there, I said it) to play during the World Cup, you’ll want to scroll down. I’ve got the definitive list of football-themed games right here, and some of them are free and raring to go right now.

Let’s say it’s a while until your team plays, you’ve already been knocked out, or your World Cup fantasy venture isn’t going the way you’d like. Why not relax (kind of) and explore another avenue by playing a football-themed game or two? Here’s the spread you need to make sure you’re capturing every soccer video game opportunity out there.

FIFA World Cup Launch Edition – Netflix

Netflix has opened up a fresh road into football-related games with FIFA World Cup Launch Edition, a new experience that’s free for anyone with a Netflix subscription and a mobile device. It’s a short-form, multiplayer-focused game that can be played in your living room or on the go via the Netflix desktop application, and it’s probably the newest World Cup game out there.

EA Sports FC 26 – Electronic Arts

It wouldn’t be a recommendation-based list for the world’s top football games without EA Sports FC getting a spot. Once known simply as FIFA, EA Sports FC 26 remains the top-tier offering in the soccer gaming space, and right now, you can leap headfirst into a tried and tested World Cup mode that features the full roster of 48 teams.

FC Mobile 26 – Electronic Arts

What happens if you’re out on the move and want to enjoy some EA Sports FC 26 action? Well, you resort to FC Mobile 26, of course! At the start of June, EA introduced the ‘World’s Game’ mode to FC Mobile 26, which invites players to assume control of one of 50 national teams and take part in a special themed event.

Free Fire – Garena

This is more of an honorable mention, but the incredibly popular battle royale game, Free Fire, is hosting the Fire Kickoff event now. The developer has bestowed a soccer-themed update across the game’s ecosystem, all in line with the World Cup 2026 tournament. There are fresh challenges to undertake, football jersey collectibles, and some maps have received football-themed makeovers.

Football Manager 26 – Sports Interactive

If you’re more of a strategy fan with a mind for tactics, facts, and figures, then you’ll want to dive into Football Manager 26, arguably the most complex football game to play during the World Cup. In May, the team at Sports Interactive unveiled the International Management update, bringing in a fully licensed World Cup experience for all players to enjoy.

Rocket League – Epic Games

It’s another honorable mention, but if you want a high-octane, energetic, and free game to play during the World Cup that has a football-centric theme to it, you can’t overlook Rocket League. This game is available on basically every platform, won’t cost you a penny, and offers extremely entertaining car-based soccer action. Oh, and there’s a limited-time World Cup event to enjoy, too.

Rematch – Sloclap

Rematch is another super energetic game that offers satisfying, short-form matches with a five-vs-five structure. It’s an online game where you’re given sole command of an individual player in a team, and collaboration means the ultimate separation between winning and losing. On June 11th, a ‘Nations Cup’ mode kicked off.

Goals – Goals AB

Goals is an honorable mention of sorts, as while it lands on this list for the obvious fact that it’s a football game, it doesn’t have any links to the World Cup. Or, at least, it didn’t have any connection at the time of writing. Goals is another free-to-play game with fast-paced matches, so it’s no risk to give it a try!

eFootball – Konami

As a chief competitor to EA Sports FC 26, eFootball, a free-to-play title, is a great shout for a soccer-based game to play during the World Cup. It doesn’t have much in the way of a link to the World Cup 2026, but it’s still a great game that costs nothing to get involved, and it’s a cross-platform title to boot (no pun intended).

Angry Birds 2 – Rovio

Let’s cap this list off with another outsider that you wouldn’t expect to make the cut. In Angry Birds 2, you’ll find that there’s a special World Cup event live now called the Season of Football, and it boasts stacks of soccer-themed challenges and exclusive rewards. It’s also free and bundles of fun outside of the limited-time event.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’ve got a better game to play during the World Cup!

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Modern Warfare 4 will have campaign early access

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/best-football-games-world-cup-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: DOJ Approves Paramount’s $111 Billion Warner Bros. Deal
item.content:encoded:
The United States Department of Justice has officially approved Paramount’s bid to purchase Warner Bros. Discovery for $111 billion.

After engaging in a bidding war with Netflix and facing multiple offer declines, Paramount secured a deal to purchase Warner Bros. in February 2026. The deal reportedly includes a cash purchase price of $31.00 per WBD share and a daily ticking fee of $0.25 per quarter starting after September 30, 2026. There was also a $7 billion safety-net regulatory termination fee if the deal doesn’t close due to regulatory issues.

At the time, Netflix, which seemed poised to secure WB, called Paramount’s offer superior. At the time, representatives at the streaming giant said, “The transaction we negotiated would have created shareholder value with a clear path to regulatory approval. However, we’ve always been disciplined, and at the price required to match Paramount Skydance’s latest offer, the deal is no longer financially attractive, so we are declining to match the Paramount Skydance bid.”

What Does This Paramount-WBD Deal Mean for Gaming?

This deal, confirmed by Deadline, will see the merger of the Paramount+ and HBO Max streaming services. Both media conglomerates have several gaming-related projects in the works. Paramount recently launched a gaming studio and is working on a Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles game based on The Last Ronin. Warner Bros. owns the IP for Harry Potter, Game of Thrones, and the entire DC Comics library. Recently, a Paramount gaming executive commented on which IP the studio sees as its bread and butter.

While SpongeBob SquarePants is great, the merging of worlds means that Batman and Superman could find themselves in Bikini Bottom one day for a video game adventure. Paramount recently quietly dropped the animated Among Us series from its streaming platform. Learn more about that here.

Insider Gaming will report any further updates to this story. What do you think about this development? Can you see any fun gaming projects coming out of it?

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/doj-approves-paramounts-111-billion-warner-bros-deal/
